title: User Federation vs Identity Brokering
description:
## Two Ways to Bring in External Users

Keycloak rarely owns *all* your users. Two distinct features connect it to the outside world, and they are easy to confuse.

### User Federation — connect to an external user *store*

Keycloak reaches **out to a backing store of user records** so those users behave like realm users. Built-in providers cover **LDAP** and **Active Directory**; the **User Storage SPI** lets you write a custom provider for any database or legacy system.

- **Where the users live:** in the external store (Keycloak queries it).
- **Import mode:** users can be **imported** (a local copy is kept and periodically **synced**) or accessed **read-only / on-demand** without a local copy.
- **Mappers** translate external attributes/groups into Keycloak attributes and roles.
- **Credentials** are typically validated by the external store (e.g. an LDAP bind).

> Think: *"these are still **our** users; they just happen to be stored in AD/LDAP."*

### Identity Brokering — delegate login to an external *IdP*

Keycloak acts as a **broker**: it doesn't store or check the credentials at all. It **redirects the user to an external Identity Provider** to authenticate, then trusts the assertion that comes back.

- **Protocols:** **OIDC** and **SAML** IdPs, plus built-in **social** providers (Google, GitHub, Microsoft...).
- **First-login flow:** on the first brokered login Keycloak can **create a local account** or **link** to an existing one.
- **Mappers** map incoming claims/assertions to Keycloak attributes and roles.

> Think: *"authentication happens **somewhere else**; we just trust them and broker the result."*

### Side by side

| | **User Federation** | **Identity Brokering** |
|---|---|---|
| Connects to | External user **store** (LDAP/AD, custom SPI) | External **IdP** (OIDC/SAML/social) |
| Who verifies credentials | The external store (or imported copy) | The external IdP |
| User records | Appear in the realm (imported or read-on-demand) | Created/linked on first login |
| Typical use | Corporate AD/LDAP directory | Partner SSO, social login, B2B |
| Sync | Yes (import + sync policies) | No store sync; per-login federation |

### Choosing

- Employees in **your** corporate **Active Directory**? → **User Federation (LDAP/AD)**.
- Let a **partner company's** users sign in with **their** SAML/OIDC IdP? → **Identity Brokering**.
- "Sign in with Google"? → **Identity Brokering** (a social IdP).
